topic: Telluric ~12 Hz peak — c7 verification of c6 minor patch (ISS-2186 "fit within" -> "follow the ... progression")
change_summary: Decider commit 5ab70a1 patched ISS-2186 by changing "fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula" to "follow the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity progression, and none corresponds to 11.7 Hz" in detail_evidence.
our_argument_summary: WIN-009 (Telluric ~12 Hz peak) remains Refuted by Data after c6 patch. The 11–12 Hz region is an inter-harmonic minimum between Schumann fundamental 7.83 Hz and first harmonic 14.3 Hz; the robust 14.3 Hz harmonic falsifies any resonance ceiling below it. The c6 patch successfully softened the over-precise "fit within" wording to "follow the ... progression" and added "none corresponds to 11.7 Hz" as a strengthening clause. The new wording correctly characterises the relationship (loss-corrected spherical-cavity progression matches measured 7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz; see WIN-029 for loss-correction citations Sentman 1990 and Mushtak & Williams 2002). No quote-mine vector remains in the patched text.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Per prior cycles c3-c5, monitor.py Domain 16 hardcodes both predicted (~11.788) and observed (~11.787) values with no live fetch. raw-text/monitor.py is not present in the current clone (ISS-2187 carry-over).
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Hardcoded — verified across multiple prior cycles.
data_sources_used: hardcoded constants in monitor.py per prior cycles
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: None identified — the check is a self-comparison of two static constants.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — patch did not touch code_analysis claims.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carries forward from c6 — no patch touched code_analysis. Domain 16 (08-live-power.txt) is the narrative description; prior cycles directly examined monitor.py and confirmed both predicted and observed values are static constants with no API call. raw-text/monitor.py remains absent from the clone (ISS-2187).
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c6. Inter-harmonic spectral minima between Schumann modes are predicted by standard spherical Earth-ionosphere cavity physics (Chave & Jones 2012, DOI 10.1017/CBO9781139020138, cited in detail_evidence). The dome relabels the gap minimum as a peak. The c6->c7 patch tightened the harmonic-progression wording but did not alter the relabeling classification.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c6. Measured Schumann harmonics (7.83, 14.3, 20.8, 27.3, 33.8 Hz) and the inter-harmonic minimum near 11 Hz are decades-old MT/ELF literature. Dome source (raw-text/02-wins.txt:78) explicitly cites "Multiple independent MT literature sources".
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c6. The f = c/(2D) formula reproduces ~11.787 Hz only by silently substituting Earth's polar diameter (~12,716 km); dome-specific parameters (firmament 9,086 km → 16.50 Hz; disc radius 20,015 km → 7.49 Hz) do not produce the target.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c6 Hole 1: raw-text/monitor.py remains absent from the curmudgeon clone (raw-text/ contains 01-home.txt through 08-live-power.txt only). The c3-c5 code-analysis claims about Domain 16 hardcoded values cannot be re-verified in subsequent clones. Already tracked as ISS-2187 (open, assigned to tinker/poller). Not blocking on WIN-009 itself — code claims are well-established across prior cycles.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action — ISS-2187 already tracks this as an infra issue. Decider should not reopen on WIN-009.

kernel_of_truth.description: Unchanged from c6 — dome correctly identifies the ~11–12 Hz region as physically distinctive (data/wins.json:WIN-009.detail_evidence). It IS distinctive: it sits between the Schumann fundamental and first harmonic, exactly where standard spherical-cavity theory predicts a power minimum (Chave & Jones 2012, cited inline; reinforced by WIN-029 loss-corrected eigenfrequency analysis).
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The dome inverts a minimum to a peak. The c6->c7 patch made this clearer by adding "none corresponds to 11.7 Hz" and by softening the harmonic-progression language to avoid quote-mine. The 14.3 Hz first harmonic still falsifies any sub-14 Hz "resonance ceiling" (data/wins.json:WIN-009.detail_verdict_text).

deeper_analysis: Advocate-mode check: a dome defender could attempt three lines of attack on the patched text. (1) "Your harmonics 7.83/14.3/20.8/27.3/33.8 Hz don't follow a simple n×7.83 progression — they deviate by 5-11%." Reply: the spherical-cavity progression is the loss-corrected eigenfrequency formula f_n = c·sqrt(n(n+1))/(2πR) plus dispersion terms (Sentman 1990; Mushtak & Williams 2002); the deviation IS the progression. The patched wording "follow the ... progression" is technically correct under standard cavity-mode theory; WIN-029 carries the explicit citations. (2) "Local MT spectra sometimes show ~12 Hz peaks from geological coupling." Reply: regional MT noise is not a global cosmological resonance; the dome claims a universal feature, not site-specific coupling. (3) "What about Schumann sub-resonances or split modes?" Reply: the canonical Earth-ionosphere cavity does not produce a resonance at 11.7 Hz; any split-mode behavior would be sub-Hz fine structure on the 7.83 Hz fundamental, not a separate peak in the inter-harmonic gap. All three defender lines fail. Cross-WIN consistency: WIN-008 (companion, 11.7 Hz cutoff) and WIN-029 (eigenfrequency formula) carry the same physics and remain mutually consistent post-patch.

citations_verified: Chave & Jones 2012 DOI 10.1017/CBO9781139020138 (verified in prior cycles)
citations_unchecked: Sentman 1990 and Mushtak & Williams 2002 — referenced via WIN-029 cross-reference; not re-verified this cycle

summary_for_decider: PATCH VERIFICATION (qid=398, decider commit 5ab70a1): c6 ISS-2186 patch landed cleanly. "fit within the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity formula" -> "follow the spherical Earth–ionosphere cavity progression, and none corresponds to 11.7 Hz". The replacement is technically accurate (loss-corrected cavity progression matches measured harmonics) and adds a strengthening clause that closes the dome-defender quote-mine vector. No new holes introduced. Verdict Refuted by Data continues to hold. Only carry-over is ISS-2187 (raw-text/monitor.py absence), already open and assigned to tinker — no decider action needed.
